title: try/catch basics
instruction:
Wrap code that might throw in try. Catch the exception type.

Run this.
expectedOutput: Error: Index was outside the bounds of the array.
successMessage: The exception is caught, handled, and execution continues. Without try/catch, the program would crash.

title: Multiple catch blocks
instruction:
Catch different exception types in order, most specific first.

Run this.
successMessage: Catch blocks are checked in order — most specific first. The generic Exception at the end catches anything else.

title: finally block
instruction:
`finally` runs whether or not an exception occurred. Use it for cleanup.

Run this.
successMessage: finally is for cleanup code — closing files, releasing resources. It runs even if an exception is not caught or if the catch re-throws.

title: Throwing exceptions
instruction:
Use `throw` to raise an exception.

Run this.
successMessage: throw creates and raises an exception with a descriptive message. The caller's catch block handles it.

title: Custom exceptions
instruction:
Create custom exception types for domain-specific errors.

Run this.
successMessage: Custom exceptions carry domain data. Callers can catch the specific type and access the Amount property.

title: Exception filters with when
instruction:
`catch (Exception ex) when (condition)` adds a filter to catch blocks.

Run this.
successMessage: when filters apply an additional condition. The 404 exception is caught by the first block, others by the second.

title: Re-throwing exceptions
instruction:
Use `throw;` (no argument) to re-throw preserving the stack trace.

Run this.
successMessage: throw; (no argument) re-throws the caught exception with the original stack trace intact. throw ex; would reset the stack trace — avoid it.

title: Exception properties
instruction:
Exception has useful properties: Message, StackTrace, InnerException.

Run this.
successMessage: InnerException chains exceptions. The outer exception wraps the inner with additional context. ?. safely handles null InnerException.
